message: agent1: working on 'Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion' - will modify src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and possibly src/click/core.py (Context.__exit__ / make_context). Will coordinate before touching core.py further.

agent2: implementing validate_nesting=False param on Context (RuntimeError on close() with open children) in src/click/core.py and src/click/shell_completion.py. Will touch Context.__init__, _make_sub_context, close/__exit__, and _resolve_context in shell_completion.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent1: ack. My fix (resource leak / issue 2644) only touches shell_completion.py's _resolve_context (wrap cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with ... as ctx:' blocks per upstream PR pallets/click#2800) plus a docstring-only tweak in core.py's Context.exit() (no behavior change). I'm applying this now - should take a couple minutes. I will NOT touch Context.__init__, close(), or __exit__ logic, so your validate_nesting work on those should be safe to layer on top. Once I'm done I'll paste the new _resolve_context so you can rebase your changes onto it instead of the old version.

Ack — heads up, I already edited _resolve_context in shell_completion.py to add validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context calls (root ctx_args, non-chain branch, chain branch). When you paste your rewritten version I'll reapply that on top. Also FYI I added a fix in core.py's Group.to_info_dict (around the sub_ctx = ctx._make_sub_context(command) call) to discard sub_ctx from ctx._open_child_contexts after use, since that context is intentionally never closed (cleanup=False) — shouldn't conflict with your exit()-docstring-only change but flagging in case you touch that area too.

agent1: done. Final _resolve_context in shell_completion.py (already on disk in /workspace/repo) is the corrected upstream version — key detail: inside each 'with cmd.make_context(...) as sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx:' block, reassign ctx/sub_ctx to the new value BEFORE reading its args (i.e. 'ctx = sub_ctx; args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args', not the other order) — an earlier draft PR upstream (pallets/click#2800) had this backwards and read stale args from the parent, #2767 fixed it. My core.py change is only the exit() docstring wording (lines ~704-709), nothing functional. Go ahead and layer validate_nesting=False onto the 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context, and your Group.to_info_dict/_open_child_contexts work in core.py — no overlap with what I touched. I'm finalizing patch.txt now.

Quick check: we appear to be in separate working copies (my disk still shows the old _resolve_context, no docstring change in __exit__ either) — the cooperation channel is text-only, not a shared filesystem. Could you paste your final _resolve_context function body (and the __exit__ docstring diff) so I can merge it into my copy alongside my validate_nesting=False additions? I'll fold it in and finalize my own patch.txt independently.
